course_title: MRI Pulse Sequence Construction & Image Contrast

learning_outcomes.description: Create MRI sequences by applying knowledge of magnetic field gradients and RF pulses.
Predict the effect of adjusting sequence parameters on image contrast.
Explain the performance of advanced sequences in producing the desired contrast.
Understand the practical implementation and limitations of imaging sequences.
Recognise and explain image artefacts and suggest approaches for their correction.

assessments.due_date:
Quiz 1 - Entry test 7/08/2026 2:00 pm

Quiz 2 covering Module 2-4 21/08/2026 2:00 pm

Quiz 3 covering Module 5 and 6 4/09/2026 2:00 pm

Quiz 4 covering Module 7 and 8 18/09/2026 2:00 pm

Quiz 5 covering Module 9 and10 16/10/2026 2:00 pm

Each quiz is designed to cover the material of two modules, except Quiz 1, which tests fundamental MRI concepts. Quiz 1 will be available at the start of the course, and the other quizzes will be released before the lecture on the first module and due by 2:00 PM on the Friday following the lecture for the second module.
assessments.task_description: Students are required to complete five online quizzes.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students will submit their answers online. Only one attempt is allowed for each quiz.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description: Students will review practical demonstrations and related modules, answering questions related to the demonstration. A similarity check will be conducted on the submissions, and the similarity report will be released after the due date.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students are required to submit their answers using the TurnItIn link available on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The End of Semester Examination will be an in-person, paper-based exam for all students.

Internally enrolled students

The end-of-semester exam will be an in-person, paper-based exam. Students will sit the exam at the St Lucia Campus.

Externally enrolled students

The end-of-semester exam will be an in-person, paper-based exam. UQ Examinations will contact students directly to confirm arrangements. You will either sit the exam at the St Lucia campus (if you live within 200km radius of the campus) or will be directed to nominate an exam centre by a certain date. 

Please ensure you update your semester address in Si-net at the start of the semester.

One reminder will be sent and students who fail to nominate an exam centre and will be automatically assigned to the St Lucia campus. Late nominations will not be accepted.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Module 1 - Introduction: Pulse Sequence Revision.

Module 1 reviews the fundamental concepts of MRI techniques as introduced in MRES7100. Students are required to study the materials on the Blackboard website to familiarise themselves with these concepts.
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 1 - Introduction: Pulse Sequence Revision. Module 1 reviews the fundamental concepts of MRI techniques as introduced in MRES7100. Students are required to study the materials on the Blackboard website to familiarise themselves with these concepts.

learning_activities.topic:
Module 2 - RF Pulses: Function, Design, Calibration.

Module 2 covers the functions of radiofrequency (RF) pulses in MRI sequences. The practical aspects, such as Specific Absorption Rate (SAR) and flip angle calibration, of RF pulses will also be covered.
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 2 - RF Pulses: Function, Design, Calibration. Module 2 covers the functions of radiofrequency (RF) pulses in MRI sequences. The practical aspects, such as Specific Absorption Rate (SAR) and flip angle calibration, of RF pulses will also be covered.

learning_activities.topic:
Module 3 - Pulsed Gradients: Balancing the Equation.

Module 3 explains the magnetic field gradients and theirs role in slice selection, and phase and frequency encoding. It will also explore the spatial encoding of MRI signals.
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 3 - Pulsed Gradients: Balancing the Equation. Module 3 explains the magnetic field gradients and theirs role in slice selection, and phase and frequency encoding. It will also explore the spatial encoding of MRI signals.

learning_activities.topic:
Module 4 - Sequence Construction: Contrast Formation and Spatial Encoding.

Module 4 will cover the construction of a conventional gradient-echo and a spin-echo sequence.
learning_activities.topic_title: Module 4 - Sequence Construction: Contrast Formation and Spatial Encoding.
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 4 - Sequence Construction: Contrast Formation and Spatial Encoding. Module 4 will cover the construction of a conventional gradient-echo and a spin-echo sequence.

learning_activities.topic:
Workshop 1 - Sequence Construction Demonstration.

In this workshop, we will demonstrate the basic process of constructing a sequence.
learning_activities.topic_description: Workshop 1 - Sequence Construction Demonstration. In this workshop, we will demonstrate the basic process of constructing a sequence.

learning_activities.topic:
Module 5 - Parallel Imaging

Module 4 covers two parallel imaging techniques: SENSE and GRAPPA. It will also explore their impacts on acquisition time and signal-to-noise ratio (SNR).
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 5 - Parallel Imaging Module 4 covers two parallel imaging techniques: SENSE and GRAPPA. It will also explore their impacts on acquisition time and signal-to-noise ratio (SNR).

learning_activities.topic:
Workshop 2 - Quiz 2 Review.

In this workshop, we will review the questions from Quiz 2.
learning_activities.topic_description: Workshop 2 - Quiz 2 Review. In this workshop, we will review the questions from Quiz 2.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 6 To Week 8(31 Aug - 20 Sep)
learning_activities.topic:
Practical Demonstration: DIXON imaging

This one-hour demonstration will showcase the Dixon sequence for fat imaging on a 3T scanner at the Centre for Advanced Imaging (CAI), UQ. The time for the demonstration session will depend on the availability of the scanner and the radiographer, and this will be communicated to the students during the semester. Students are encouraged to observe the demonstration in person at CAI. Recordings of the demonstration will be uploaded to the Blackboard website. Questions for the mid-semester assignment will be based on this demonstration.
learning_activities.topic_description: Practical Demonstration: DIXON imaging This one-hour demonstration will showcase the Dixon sequence for fat imaging on a 3T scanner at the Centre for Advanced Imaging (CAI), UQ. The time for the demonstration session will depend on the availability of the scanner and the radiographer, and this will be communicated to the students during the semester. Students are encouraged to observe the demonstration in person at CAI. Recordings of the demonstration will be uploaded to the Blackboard website. Questions for the mid-semester assignment will be based on this demonstration.

learning_activities.topic:
Module 6 - 3D Imaging. Multi-echoes: Dixon, MEDIC.

This module will cover the difference between 2-D and 3-D imaging sequences, the chemical shift of fat, Dixon's method and multi-echo gradient echo acquisition.
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 6 - 3D Imaging.  Multi-echoes: Dixon, MEDIC. This module will cover the difference between 2-D and 3-D imaging sequences, the chemical shift of fat, Dixon's method and multi-echo gradient echo acquisition.

learning_activities.topic:
Module 7 - GRE: Steady State - Hahn, DESS, FISP

This module will address the establishment of steady state in imaging. It will discuss three gradient echo sequences: Spoiled, Balanced, and Refocused. The module will also explore the formation of both Hahn echo and Stimulated echo.
learning_activities.topic_title: Module 7 - GRE: Steady State - Hahn, DESS, FISP
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 7 - GRE:  Steady State - Hahn, DESS, FISP This module will address the establishment of steady state in imaging. It will discuss three gradient echo sequences: Spoiled, Balanced, and Refocused. The module will also explore the formation of both Hahn echo and Stimulated echo.

learning_activities.topic:
Workshop 3 - Quiz 3 Review

We will review the questions from Quiz 3 in the workshop.
learning_activities.topic_description: Workshop 3 - Quiz 3 Review We will review the questions from Quiz 3 in the workshop.

learning_activities.topic:
Module 8 - GRE: CISS, Feel the VIBE

The module examines two 3D fast steady-state gradient echo sequences: VIBE ad CISS.
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 8 - GRE: CISS,  Feel the VIBE The module examines two 3D fast steady-state gradient echo sequences: VIBE ad CISS.

learning_activities.topic:
Module 9 - Turbocharged: MPRAGE or MP2RAGE?

This module will discuss three frequently-used imaging sequences: TurboFLASH, MPRAGE and MP2RAGE.
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 9 - Turbocharged: MPRAGE or MP2RAGE? This module will discuss three frequently-used imaging sequences: TurboFLASH, MPRAGE and MP2RAGE.

learning_activities.topic:
Workshop 4 - Quiz 4 Review.

We will review the questions from Quiz 4 in the workshop.
learning_activities.topic_description: Workshop 4 - Quiz 4 Review. We will review the questions from Quiz 4 in the workshop.

learning_activities.topic:
Module 10 - FLAIR, DIR. Making HASTE.

This module will cover fast spin-echo sequences, including fast spin echo (FSE), HASTE, SPACE and FLAIR. Dual inversion recovery method will also be introduced.
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 10 - FLAIR, DIR. Making HASTE. This module will cover fast spin-echo sequences, including fast spin echo (FSE), HASTE, SPACE and FLAIR. Dual inversion recovery method will also be introduced.

learning_activities.topic:
Module 11 - Spotting trouble: Static and Motion Artefacts.

This module examines some of the most common static artefacts on MRI images, discussing their origin, manifestation, and potential remedy methods.
learning_activities.topic_title: Module 11 - Spotting trouble: Static and Motion Artefacts.
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 11 - Spotting trouble: Static and Motion Artefacts. This module examines some of the most common static artefacts on MRI images, discussing their origin, manifestation, and potential remedy methods.
